name: Hot Eggs & Shredded Vegetable Salad
recipeIngredient: 1/4 teaspoon Nuoc Mam or light soy sauce
1 small carrot, peeled and thinly sliced
1 small daikon, peeled and thinly sliced
1 small cucumber, peeled, seeded, and thinly sliced
1 small green bell pepper, thinly sliced
1 small red bell pepper, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the vermicelli in warm water for 30 minutes, then drain and cut into 2-inch lengths.
2. Soak the dried mushrooms in warm water for 30 minutes, discard the stems, and slice thinly.
3. Beat together the eggs, soy sauce, black pepper, and water. Heat oil over moderate heat. Pour in half of the egg mixture and tilt the pan to spread evenly over the bottom. Cook for 30 seconds, then turn and cook for another 15 seconds. Repeat with the remaining mixture. Cool and slice into thin strips.
4. Toss the sesame seeds in a dry pan over moderate heat until golden brown. Set aside.
5. Combine garlic, salt, rice vinegar, sugar, soy sauce, and sesame oil in a small bowl. Set aside.
6. Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook vermicelli, snow peas, bamboo shoots, and mushrooms for 30 minutes. Drain into a colander and run under cold water. Dry and set aside.
7. Mix carrots, daikon, cucumber, bell peppers, celery, and red onion with salt. Toss well and let stand for 30 minutes. Rinse the vegetables with cold water and squeeze out excess moisture. Add sesame seeds.
8. Combine shredded vegetables with blanched vegetables in a large bowl. Sprinkle the sauce over the mixture. Toss well. Transfer to a serving platter. Sprinkle with black pepper and garnish with egg pancake strips, cilantro sprigs, and ground peanuts.
